title: ophrys lutea subsp. subfusca
intro: Ophrys lutea subsp. subfusca, commonly known as the yellow bee orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and distinctive shape. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The yellow bee orchid is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the flowers and roots of the yellow bee orchid can be used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
